title: MindWatch
subtitle: Multimodal Seizure Prediction
short: MindWatch is a deep learning-based multimodal seizure prediction framework that analyzes EEG, ECG, EMG, and motion signals collected from wearable monitoring systems. The system combines spatial feature extraction and temporal modelling to identify seizure-related physiological patterns.
problem: Drug-resistant epilepsy patients may experience seizures without sufficient warning. Single-signal monitoring systems can miss important physiological changes, while severe class imbalance can cause models to favor normal states and increase false alarms.
solution: MindWatch combines multiple physiological modalities within one prediction framework. CNN models extract spatial and hidden representations from physiological signals, while BiLSTM networks capture temporal dependencies across consecutive time windows. The framework also applies data-level preprocessing and model-level imbalance handling to improve minority-class detection and reduce false predictions.

models.title: Multimodal Deep Learning
models.description: Combined EEG, ECG, EMG, and motion signals within one seizure-prediction framework.

models.title: Model Architecture
models.description: CNN for physiological feature extraction and BiLSTM for temporal pattern modelling.

models.title: Class Imbalance Handling
models.description: LDAM-DRW, Weighted Random Sampling, Weighted Cross-Entropy, and Focal Loss.

models.title: Signal Processing and Development
models.description: Python, PyTorch, MNE, NumPy, Pandas, and Scikit-learn.

results: The best-performing configuration achieved 97.99% balanced accuracy, 89.70% Macro F1-score, and a 3.23% pre-ictal false-positive rate using LDAM-DRW with Weighted Random Sampling.
